content.explanation:
**Want To / Wanted To — Expressing Desire:**

**WANT TO (Present Desire — अभी चाहना):**
Structure: Subject + want/wants + to + V1

🇮🇳 मैं English fluent बोलना चाहता हूँ।
🇬🇧 I **want to** speak English fluently.

He/She/It → **wants to** + V1
I/You/We/They → **want to** + V1

**WANTED TO (Past Desire — पहले चाहता था):**
Structure: Subject + **wanted to** + V1 (same for all)

🇮🇳 मैं बचपन में cricketer बनना चाहता था।
🇬🇧 I **wanted to** become a cricketer in childhood.

**Important Patterns:**
• I want + noun: "I want a coffee."
• I want + to + V1: "I want to learn."
• I want + object + to + V1: "I want you to help me."
• I wanted + object + to + V1: "I wanted him to come."
rules: Want to + V1 = present desire (want/wants based on subject)
Wanted to + V1 = past desire (same for all subjects)
Want + object + to + V1: "I want you to stay."
Want is a stative verb — never use "wanting" or "am wanting"
Question: Do you want to...? / Did you want to...?
Negative: do not want to / did not want to
content.memoryTrick:
**Want = Direct Desire Express करना**
Want TO + verb = मेरी खुद की इच्छा
Want + someone + TO + verb = किसी और की इच्छा करना
Wanting = कभी नहीं (stative verb है!)

examples.hindi: मैं एक नई गाड़ी खरीदना चाहता हूँ।
examples.english: I want to buy a new car.
She does not want to come here.

examples.hindi: क्या तुम कुछ खाना चाहते हो?
examples.english: Do you want to eat something?

examples.hindi: मैं चाहता हूँ कि तुम यहाँ रहो।
examples.english: I want you to stay here.

examples.hindi: मैं पहले IAS बनना चाहता था।
examples.english: I wanted to become an IAS officer.

examples.hindi: उसने चाहा था कि meeting रद्द हो।
examples.english: She wanted the meeting to be cancelled.

mistakes.why: Want = stative verb — never use am/is/are + wanting
Want + object + to + V1 (not 'want that + clause')

speakingTips: Daily goals: 'Today I want to complete 5 lessons and practice speaking.'
Expressing feelings: 'I wanted to tell you this earlier but...'
Professional: 'I want this project to be completed by Friday.'

vocabulary.example: My desire is to speak fluent English.
I aspire to become a great leader.
He craves recognition for his work.
She yearns to travel the world.
His ambition is to start his own company.
I want to pursue my passion.

practice.hindi: मैं इंजीनियर बनना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to become an engineer.
alternatives: i wish to become an engineer.

practice.hindi: वह विदेश में पढ़ना चाहती है।
practice.english: She wants to study abroad.
alternatives: she wishes to study overseas.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम कुछ खाना चाहते हो?
practice.english: Do you want to eat something?
alternatives: would you like to have something?

practice.hindi: वह नई भाषा सीखना चाहता है।
practice.english: He wants to learn a new language.
alternatives: he wishes to learn another language.

practice.hindi: हम एक साथ छुट्टी मनाना चाहते हैं।
practice.english: We want to celebrate the holiday together.
alternatives: we want to go on vacation together.

practice.hindi: मुझे पानी पीना है।
practice.english: I want to drink water.
alternatives: i need some water.

practice.hindi: वह एक अच्छा इंसान बनना चाहती है।
practice.english: She wants to be a good person.
alternatives: she wishes to become a good human being.

practice.hindi: क्या वह अभी सोना चाहता है?
practice.english: Does he want to sleep now?
alternatives: does he feel like sleeping?
practice.hint: Does he want to + base verb ?

practice.hindi: मैं तुमसे बात करना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to talk to you.
alternatives: i want to speak with you.

practice.hindi: वे नई कंपनी खोलना चाहते हैं।
practice.english: They want to open a new company.
alternatives: they wish to start a new business.

practice.hindi: मैं डॉक्टर बनना चाहता था।
practice.english: I wanted to become a doctor.
alternatives: i had wished to be a doctor.

practice.hindi: वह क्रिकेटर बनना चाहती थी।
practice.english: She wanted to become a cricketer.
alternatives: she had wished to be a cricketer.

practice.hindi: हम उससे मिलना चाहते थे लेकिन वह व्यस्त था।
practice.english: We wanted to meet him but he was busy.
alternatives: we had wanted to see him but he was occupied.

practice.hindi: मैं उससे माफ़ी माँगना चाहता था।
practice.english: I wanted to apologize to him.
alternatives: i had wanted to say sorry to him.

practice.hindi: वह बचपन से पायलट बनना चाहता था।
practice.english: He had wanted to become a pilot since childhood.
alternatives: he always wanted to be a pilot.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम उससे मिलना चाहते थे?
practice.english: Did you want to meet him?
alternatives: had you wanted to see him?

practice.hindi: उसने मुझे बताया कि वह जाना चाहती थी।
practice.english: She told me that she wanted to go.
alternatives: she said she had wanted to leave.
practice.hint: told me that + wanted to + go

practice.hindi: मैं उस पार्टी में नहीं जाना चाहता था।
practice.english: I did not want to go to that party.
alternatives: i didn't feel like going to that party.

practice.hindi: वे वहाँ रहना नहीं चाहते थे।
practice.english: They did not want to stay there.
alternatives: they didn't want to live there.

practice.hindi: मुझे पता था कि वह आना चाहती थी।
practice.english: I knew she wanted to come.
alternatives: i was aware that she wanted to visit.
